tests.description: sphereVolume generator returns Float32Array with length 3 × pointCount
sphereVolume generator produces non-zero spread in X, Y, and Z independently
sphereVolume generator produces points that are NOT coplanar
sphereVolume generator is deterministic — same seed produces identical output
sphereVolume generator produces different output for different seeds
shell generator returns Float32Array with length 3 × pointCount
shell generator produces non-zero spread in X, Y, and Z independently
shell generator produces non-coplanar points
shell generator is deterministic with same seed
shell generator produces different output for different seeds
torusVolume generator returns Float32Array with length 3 × pointCount
torusVolume generator produces non-zero spread in X, Y, and Z independently
torusVolume generator produces non-coplanar points
torusVolume generator is deterministic with same seed
torusVolume generator produces different output for different seeds
noiseLattice generator returns Float32Array with length 3 × pointCount
noiseLattice generator produces non-zero spread in X, Y, and Z independently
noiseLattice generator produces non-coplanar points
noiseLattice generator is deterministic with same seed
noiseLattice generator produces different output for different seeds
spiralField generator returns Float32Array with length 3 × pointCount
spiralField generator produces non-zero spread in X, Y, and Z independently
spiralField generator produces non-coplanar points
spiralField generator is deterministic with same seed
spiralField generator produces different output for different seeds
pointCount=0 returns empty Float32Array
pointCount=1 returns a valid 3D point (length 3, no NaN)
all generators produce points with bounding box spanning non-zero range in each axis
no NaN values in any generator output
no Infinity values in any generator output
no localStorage or cookie access during generation
init() produces volumetric geometry with Z-depth > 1.0 after generator integration
init() produces non-coplanar points after generator integration
different seeds can produce different generator shapes
determinism preserved after generator integration — same seed produces identical positions

testFiles.imports:
import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest';
import { generateVolumetricPoints } from '../../../src/visual/generators/volumetricPoints';

coverageTargets.generators: 5 shapes × 5 core assertions (length, spread, coplanarity, determinism, seed variation) = 25 tests
coverageTargets.edgeCases: pointCount=0, pointCount=1, NaN check, Infinity check, privacy = 6 tests
coverageTargets.integration: Z-depth, non-coplanar, shape variation, determinism = 4 tests
